acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Outbound sessions negotiated with `live_stream_enabled=false` never subscribe to live events or send hot-cache.
- [ ] `send_events`/`send_hot_cache` are not callable for snapshot-only sessions at compile time.
- [ ] Inbound path continues to gate live stream and remains consistent with outbound.
- [ ] Tests cover both negotiation paths (live vs snapshot) and assert no live events sent in snapshot mode.
- [ ] `cargo test` passes.
description:
**Problem**
Outbound replication ignores `peer.live_stream_enabled`. After handshake, we always subscribe to the broadcaster and send live events + hot-cache, even when the negotiated capability is false. This violates the wire contract and makes correctness depend on peer tolerance.

This is a classic invariant that the type system should encode: once a session is negotiated as snapshot-only, the compiler should make it impossible to call live-stream send paths.

**Impact**
- Protocol mismatch: peers that don’t support live streaming still receive live Events frames.
- Hard-to-test correctness risk; a refactor can silently reintroduce this since nothing is type-gated.

**Files**
- `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/repl/manager.rs` (outbound loop)
- `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/repl/session.rs` (handshake negotiation)
- `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/repl/server.rs` (inbound already gates; keep consistent)
design:
**Design (opinionated)**
Encode live-stream capability in the session typestate so the compiler prevents misuse.

1) Split streaming phase into two typestates (or a generic):
```rust
struct StreamingLive { peer: SessionPeer }
struct StreamingSnapshot { peer: SessionPeer }
// or Session<Streaming<Live>> vs Session<Streaming<SnapshotOnly>>
```
2) Handshake transition chooses the correct type based on `live_stream_enabled`.
- If `live_stream_enabled == true`, transition to `StreamingLive`.
- Else transition to `StreamingSnapshot`.

3) Only expose live-stream send helpers for `StreamingLive`:
- `send_events`, `send_hot_cache`, event subscription setup all require `&Session<*, StreamingLive>`.
- `StreamingSnapshot` still supports `Want` handling.

4) Outbound loop must respect typestate:
- Do not create broadcaster subscription unless session is `StreamingLive`.
- No pending-event draining/hot-cache send in snapshot-only mode.

**Ordering / invariants preserved**
- Live-stream mode preserves existing ordering semantics (round-robin/wal ordering unchanged).
- Snapshot-only mode only sends WANT responses; ordering is still canonical via WAL/Want logic.
title: Outbound repl must enforce live_stream_enabled via typestate

description: During sync retry tests, push_update_reference returns 'failed to lock file' which is currently treated as PushRejected instead of NonFastForward. Treat lockfile failures as retryable so sync_with_retry can retry.
title: Sync push lockfile errors should be retryable

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A direct convenience syntax for common blocking-dependency creation exists at `bd dep ... --blocks ...` or an equally ergonomic equivalent.
- [ ] Help text explains how the shorthand maps onto canonical dependency semantics.
- [ ] Common dep aliases/convenience naming are covered where they materially improve agent workflows.
- [ ] Tests cover parse behavior and the resulting dependency direction.
description:
**Problem**
Dependency management in beads-rs is more ceremonial than beads-go. beads-go supports a convenience form like `bd dep <blocker> --blocks <blocked>` and offers a more ergonomic top-level dep surface for common workflows. This matters for agents because dependency edits are frequent and should be low-friction.

**Files:** crates/beads-cli/src/cli.rs, crates/beads-cli/src/commands/dep.rs, associated parser/help tests
design: Add a convenience dep entry path that preserves canonical semantics while reducing typing and mental overhead. The main desire path is a direct `bd dep <id> --blocks <id>` style workflow, with help text that clearly maps it to the underlying `dep add` relationship so semantics remain unambiguous.
title: Add P1 dependency convenience UX to
